message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements for proper cleanup. Need core.py for Context.__exit__ support and shell_completion.py for the fix itself. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature: Add Nested Context Validation (validate_nesting param on Context). Need to edit core.py (Context class) and shell_completion.py (disable validation during completion). What files does your feature touch? If you also need core.py/shell_completion.py, let's figure out how to split by section/function.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Our features collide on the same 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context. Proposal: you own shell_completion.py entirely (your with-statement wrapping), I own core.py entirely (Context.validate_nesting logic - __enter__/__exit__/close already exist, I'll extend close()). Since you're already touching those 3 make_context() call sites for the with-wrapping, please also add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each of those 3 calls (cli.make_context(...) and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls) so shell completion opts out of my new validation. That's the only thing I need in shell_completion.py. OK with this split?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Overlap check: my fix only needs shell_completion.py's _resolve_context (wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements) — no core.py changes needed since Context already has __enter__/__exit__/close(). Proposal: I own shell_completion.py entirely, you own core.py entirely. For your validate_nesting feature: since _resolve_context already passes resilient_parsing=True to all make_context calls, could you make Context automatically skip nesting validation when resilient_parsing=True (in core.py only)? That way you never need to touch shell_completion.py and we stay fully disjoint. If you truly need a hook in shell_completion.py, tell me the exact line/kwarg and I'll add it for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each. You own core.py entirely for the Context.validate_nesting feature. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Accepted: I own core.py, you own shell_completion.py. Please avoid the resilient_parsing shortcut - my task spec explicitly requires shell_completion.py to set validate_nesting=False explicitly, not infer it from resilient_parsing. Exact instructions for _resolve_context (around line 546-582 currently): (1) right after 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True', add 'ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False' so it flows into cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args); (2) in 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' add ', validate_nesting=False'; (3) in the sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True,) call, add 'validate_nesting=False,' too. Context.make_context/make_context already just forwards extra kwargs to Context(), and validate_nesting will default to False anyway so this is purely explicit/defensive - should not conflict with your with-statement wrapping. Thanks! Sending DONE from my side once core.py is done.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Done and pushed to team/agent1. _resolve_context now wraps all 3 make_context() calls in 'with' statements (matching upstream click's actual fix) and includes validate_nesting=False in the ctx_args dict and both cmd.make_context() calls as you requested. Verified with a stub Context.__init__ that accepts validate_nesting: all 40 existing shell_completion tests pass, and a manual ResourceWarning check confirms file handles from click.File are now closed after completion. Waiting on your core.py DONE before I do the final merge.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: core.py done: Context.__init__ gains validate_nesting=False param (public attr), _make_sub_context inherits parent's validate_nesting and tracks children in self._children when enabled, close() raises RuntimeError listing open children and removes itself from parent tracking on success. Merged your team/agent1 branch cleanly (no conflicts) and ran full test suite: 614 passed, 21 skipped, 1 xfailed. Verified _resolve_context + validate_nesting integration manually too. Ready to finalize patch.txt.
